item.title: Integration credentials directory created without mode 0700 — engine/ dir world-listable
item.intent: The engine/ directory must not be world-listable. It stores integration credentials and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents cannot be enumerated, matching the protection applied to credentials.json itself.
item.description: src/secure/credentials/credential-store.ts line 60 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option, creating deployDir/engine/ with default permissions (0755). The engine/ directory contains credentials.json (integration API keys — IMAP passwords, Telegram tokens, OAuth secrets) written with mode 0600 (correct), but any user on the system can ls the engine/ directory to enumerate that integration credentials exist. Fix: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the import from defaults.js and pass { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to the mkdirSync call.
